strategy: Enter on time. Wizards who join after the second round get hit with Medusa.
Kill order: King Borr must die before Grendelynn. If Grendelynn dies first she revives/heals, so pile your damage onto Borr and hit as hard as you can.
Lead with an Indemnity-protected Feint as your very first trap. If you place any other trap first he Cleanse Wards them all off; the Indemnity Feint has to go down before the rest of your stack.
Beguile Grendelynn on the same turn as your hit. This disables her Shadow Sentinel, which otherwise Intercepts 75% of all damage aimed at King Borr, and lets your full hit land on the boss.
Never Feint him unprotected - he responds with a mass Cleanse Ward. Only use Indemnity-protected traps (or the Balance version of Feint, which does not trigger the cleanse).
Stack blades, keep the Indemnity Feint and traps up, disable the Intercept with the beguile, then burst King Borr down before you finish Grendelynn.
cheatNotes: Grendelynn shields with a 75% Intercept Sentinel, absorbing your hits aimed at King Borr.
Beguiling Grendelynn flips her Intercept to protect your team for as long as the beguile holds; a strong opener.
Joining late gets Medusa (865) cast on you; enter on time.
Kill Grendelynn before King Borr and she full-heals; keep them even and finish together.
If you drop both in the same Round the avenge and heal still fire, but with both gone it no longer matters; aim for a simultaneous kill.
Feinting him makes him Cleanse all unprotected traps off; use Balance Feint or protect traps with Indemnity.
Mass Feint also triggers the trap cleanse, so avoid it here.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger the cleanse; it is the safe way to trap him.
